title: Implement FSEvents-based directory monitoring
changes: In `startMonitoring()`, create an FSEventStream watching `self.directory` with `kFSEventStreamCreateFlagFileEvents` so we get per-file events, not just directory-level notifications
On each event, check: is the file extension `.m4a`? If not, ignore. Is the event a creation or rename-in (file appearing)? If not, ignore.
When a new .m4a file is detected, don't emit immediately — hand off to the sync completion check (Task 002.3). For now, call `onFileDetected` directly as a placeholder.
In `stopMonitoring()`, invalidate and release the FSEventStream
FSEvents requires a RunLoop or DispatchQueue — use a dedicated `DispatchQueue(label: "com.utterd.watcher")` to avoid blocking the main thread

tests.name: test_detectsNewM4AFile
tests.description: Create a temp directory, start the watcher on it, copy a .m4a file in, assert onFileDetected fires within 5 seconds with the correct path

tests.name: test_ignoresNonM4AFiles
tests.description: Copy a .txt file into the watched directory, assert onFileDetected does not fire within 2 seconds

tests.name: test_detectsMultipleFiles
tests.description: Copy two .m4a files in rapid succession, assert onFileDetected fires twice with both paths

doneWhen: Watcher detects new .m4a files in a temp directory and emits their paths. Non-.m4a files are ignored. Tests pass.
doNot: Implement sync completion detection yet — that's Task 002.3. For now, emit immediately on detection.
Filter out pre-existing files yet — that's Task 002.4
